content.assessment: This proposal works entirely within the existing centrally-graded uniform exam infrastructure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02), making it highly institutionally compatible since it requires no new legal architecture, only redesign of item types and interview weighting within the current three-track structure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01). However, the lens questions about which selection/access/governance mechanisms remain unchanged expose a major limitation: free school choice, the 4/6/8-year track structure, and the underlying legal basis for early selection are all left intact. The documented coaching market (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05) and stark parental-education gaps (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) originate partly from family capacity to prepare for the specific exam format, but exam redesign does not address the pre-existing sorting occurring in feeder primary schools, nor does it reach families whose schools do not facilitate competition entry.
opportunities: Could be layered onto the ministry's already-stated intent to review the entrance-exam system (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10)
strengths: Uses an existing, already-centralized administrative lever (uniform national exam) rather than requiring new institutional architecture (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02)
Directly targets a documented mechanism (coaching investment concentrated among university-educated parents) with a plausible institutional handle (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)
threats: Elite self-selection dynamics documented by the ministry (favorable family background driving outcomes, not added value) suggest exam redesign may not touch the underlying composition effect even if coaching-specific advantage is reduced (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08)
weaknesses: Leaves the three-track legal structure and free school choice completely unchanged, meaning the core institutional architecture generating segregation persists (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01, F-live-hungarian_education_system-03)
Does not address the documented parental-education composition gap in track access, which reflects entrenched pre-exam sorting rather than exam format alone (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04)
